title: Ulcerative Colitis
chief_complaint: I have bloody diarrhea 10-12 times a day with urgency and tenesmus. I can't leave my house. I feel like I have to go but nothing comes out except blood and mucus. This has been going on for 6 weeks.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Diffuse tenderness in lower abdomen. No masses. Bowel sounds hyperactive. No peritoneal signs.
physical_exam.Rectal: Bloody mucoid discharge. Diffuse tenderness. No perianal disease.

hidden_data.colonoscopy: Continuous, circumferential inflammation from rectum to descending colon (left-sided colitis). Diffuse erythema, granularity, loss of vascular pattern. Friable mucosa with contact bleeding. Pseudopolyps. No skip lesions.
hidden_data.biopsy: Diffuse mucosal and submucosal inflammation. Crypt abscesses and crypt architectural distortion. No granulomas. Confined to mucosa (not transmural).
hidden_data.stool: Negative for infections, C. diff negative
hidden_data.abdominal_xray: Thumbprinting in descending colon. No toxic megacolon (transverse colon <6cm).

key_learning_points: UC = mucosal inflammation limited to colon. ALWAYS involves rectum, extends proximally continuously. NO skip lesions
Crohn's vs UC: UC has rectal involvement, continuous disease, mucosal only, no granulomas, no fistulas. Crohn's has rectal sparing, skip lesions, transmural, granulomas, fistulas
Severity: Mild <4 stools/day, Moderate 4-6, Severe >6 bloody stools + systemic toxicity (fever, tachycardia, anemia, elevated CRP)

Treatment: 5-ASA (Mesalamine) first-line for mild-moderate. Prednisone for moderate-severe. Biologics (Infliximab, Adalimumab, Vedolizumab) for steroid-refractory. Tofacitinib (JAK inhibitor) for refractory
Colectomy for refractory disease, toxic megacolon, perforation, or dysplasia. UC is CURED by total proctocolectomy (unlike Crohn's)
